mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration and weak consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or directional accuracy falls below 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns when RSAFS and HOUST both trend upward, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle phases, with directional accuracy below 52% across 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3%, or the signal shows zero predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting higher wage growth and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in > 50% of observed cases, failing to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening business activity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO falls > 0.5%, or directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward tests.
